name: Prevu
description_short: Discount real estate brokerage offering full-service buyer and seller representation, with buyers earning up to 1% cash rebate at closing via the Smart Buyer™ platform.
description_long:
Prevu is a discount real estate brokerage founded in 2017 by Chase Marsh and Thomas Kutzman in New York City. The company holds standard state real estate brokerage licenses in 13 states and Washington, D.C., serving major markets including New York, Boston, Miami, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Seattle, Denver, Austin, Philadelphia, New Jersey, and Connecticut. In November 2025, Prevu was acquired by reAlpha Tech Corp. (Nasdaq: AIRE), with co-founder Thomas Kutzman appointed CEO of the combined brokerage division, reAlpha Realty. No CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications apply — Prevu is a licensed real estate brokerage, not a nonprofit or consumer finance institution.

Prevu's flagship product is the Smart Buyer™ platform, a digital-first home purchase tool that allows buyers to search properties, schedule showings, submit offers, and communicate with agents entirely online. At closing, buyers receive a rebate of up to 1% of the purchase price — Prevu collects the standard buyer's agent commission (approximately 3%) and returns roughly two-thirds to the client. The full 1% rebate is available only on homes priced above approximately $1.25M; average rebates across completed transactions have run $23,000–$25,000. For sellers, Prevu charges a 2% listing fee versus the traditional 2.5–3%. The buyer rebate is subject to lender approval and property type restrictions; minimum fees vary by market and are not publicly disclosed.

With 1,000+ completed transactions since founding, Prevu distinguishes itself by displaying the estimated rebate amount directly on each property listing before the buyer submits an offer — eliminating the opaque, end-of-deal uncertainty common in traditional brokerage. Agents are fully licensed and handle negotiation and transaction management; this is not a DIY or limited-service platform. The 2025 reAlpha acquisition is designed to eventually pair Prevu's brokerage capabilities with integrated mortgage origination, positioning the combined entity as a one-stop home purchase platform.

Prevu is best suited for buyers of higher-value properties in major metro markets who want full-service representation at reduced cost. The rebate is most meaningful on homes above $500K and maximized above $1.25M — buyers of lower-priced homes will see smaller absolute savings. Minimum fees are opaque, the rebate requires lender sign-off (a potential friction point in competitive markets), and service is limited to 13 states plus D.C. Critically, Prevu is a real estate brokerage — it does not offer mortgages, credit products, or any consumer financing, and its placement in a consumer finance directory reflects a miscategorization.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee found in research. Contact provider for details.

tiers.name: Smart Buyer™ — Buyer Representation
features: Up to 1% of purchase price returned as cash rebate at closing
Smart Buyer™ digital platform with per-listing rebate estimates

tiers.name: Seller Listing
features: Offer negotiation and transaction management through closing

pros: Buyers receive up to 1% of purchase price as a cash rebate at closing — averaging $23,000–$25,000 per completed transaction
Seller listing fee of 2% is below the traditional 2.5–3% range, potentially saving thousands at closing
Smart Buyer™ platform shows the estimated rebate on each listing upfront, before offer submission — no surprises
Full-service licensed agents in 13 states + D.C. covering most major U.S. metro markets
1,000+ completed transactions since founding in 2017 demonstrates meaningful real-world volume
Acquired by publicly traded reAlpha Tech Corp. (Nasdaq: AIRE) in November 2025, adding institutional backing and a roadmap for integrated mortgage services
cons: Full 1% buyer rebate only available on homes priced above approximately $1.25M — buyers of lower-cost properties receive a proportionally smaller rebate
Buyer rebate requires lender approval and has property type restrictions, which can complicate or delay closings in competitive markets
Minimum fees by market are not publicly disclosed on the website, making true cost comparison difficult
Service available only in 13 states + D.C. — buyers outside covered markets cannot use Prevu
No confirmed native mobile app — the Smart Buyer™ platform appears web-based only
best_for: Homebuyers purchasing properties above $500K in major U.S. metro markets who want full-service agent representation with a cash rebate at closing
Buyers of luxury or high-value homes ($1.25M+) where the full 1% rebate delivers maximum dollar savings
Sellers in covered markets looking to reduce listing commission below the traditional 2.5–3%
Tech-savvy buyers who prefer a digital-first, low-pressure home search and offer workflow over traditional in-person brokerage
diagnosis: Prevu is best for buyers and sellers in major U.S. metro markets who want full-service real estate representation at below-market commission rates — the rebate is most impactful on homes above $500K and maximized above $1.25M. The primary caveat is that this is a real estate brokerage, not a consumer finance product; buyers still require independent mortgage financing, the rebate is subject to lender approval, and Prevu offers no credit, lending, or debt services of any kind.
services: Buyer representation with Smart Buyer™ rebate (up to 1% of purchase price at closing)
Digital property search with per-listing estimated rebate display
Showing scheduling via Smart Buyer™ platform
Transaction management from offer acceptance through closing
Property type and eligibility guidance for rebate qualification
Post-acquisition integration with reAlpha mortgage services (in development)
